2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch deduplicates the lock_list_lru() function by moving it to a separate static inline function and removing its duplicate definition in the code. The shared function is used in both MEMCG and non-MEMCG paths, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability.

reviews.author: Wei Yang
reviews.summary: Suggested unifying parameter names and annotations for improved readability. Requested a follow-up patch to address this issue.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, May 27, 2026 at 04:45:12PM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:
>The MEMCG and !MEMCG paths have the same pattern. Share the code.
>
>Reviewed-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>
>Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
>Reviewed-by: Lorenzo Stoakes (Oracle) <ljs@kernel.org>
>Signed-off-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>
>Reviewed-by: Liam R. Howlett (Oracle) <liam@infradead.org>
>---
> mm/list_lru.c | 21 +++++++++------------
> 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
>
>diff --git a/mm/list_lru.c b/mm/list_lru.c
>index 7d0523e44010..fdb3fe2ea64f 100644
>--- a/mm/list_lru.c
>+++ b/mm/list_lru.c
>@@ -15,6 +15,14 @@
> #include "slab.h"
> #include "internal.h"

Hi, Johannes

One very tiny nit below.

> 
>+static inline void lock_list_lru(struct list_lru_one *l, bool irq)

Here we use @irq.

>+{
>+	if (irq)
>+		spin_lock_irq(&l->lock);
>+	else
>+		spin_lock(&l->lock);
>+}
>+
> static inline void unlock_list_lru(struct list_lru_one *l, bool irq_off)

Here we use @irq_off.

Do you think it would be nicer to unify the parameter name?

Also the name in callsite and the argument annotation. Would it be cleaner
readers?

-- 
Wei Yang
Help you, Help me

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Acknowledged Wei's suggestion and agreed to address it in a follow-up patch.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, May 29, 2026 at 09:56:28AM +0000, Wei Yang wrote:
> On Wed, May 27, 2026 at 04:45:12PM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:
> >The MEMCG and !MEMCG paths have the same pattern. Share the code.
> >
> >Reviewed-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>
> >Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
> >Reviewed-by: Lorenzo Stoakes (Oracle) <ljs@kernel.org>
> >Signed-off-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>
> >Reviewed-by: Liam R. Howlett (Oracle) <liam@infradead.org>
> >---
> > mm/list_lru.c | 21 +++++++++------------
> > 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
> >
> >diff --git a/mm/list_lru.c b/mm/list_lru.c
> >index 7d0523e44010..fdb3fe2ea64f 100644
> >--- a/mm/list_lru.c
> >+++ b/mm/list_lru.c
> >@@ -15,6 +15,14 @@
> > #include "slab.h"
> > #include "internal.h"
> 
> Hi, Johannes
> 
> One very tiny nit below.
> 
> > 
> >+static inline void lock_list_lru(struct list_lru_one *l, bool irq)
> 
> Here we use @irq.
> 
> >+{
> >+	if (irq)
> >+		spin_lock_irq(&l->lock);
> >+	else
> >+		spin_lock(&l->lock);
> >+}
> >+
> > static inline void unlock_list_lru(struct list_lru_one *l, bool irq_off)
> 
> Here we use @irq_off.
> 
> Do you think it would be nicer to unify the parameter name?

Yes, I think it would be nicer.

Note that I inherited this - we had irq on the lock and irq_off on the
unlock before already. I didn't want to mix even more yak shaving prep
patches into this series.

Mind sending a follow-up patch on top of mm-unstable?

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch deduplicates the lock_list_lru() function by removing redundant code and unifying parameter names to improve readability.

reviews.author: Wei Yang
reviews.summary: Agreed to wait for the rest of the series to settle down before addressing the suggested changes.
reviews.raw_body:

>> >Reviewed-by: Liam R. Howlett (Oracle) <liam@infradead.org>
>> >---
>> > mm/list_lru.c | 21 +++++++++------------
>> > 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
>> >
>> >diff --git a/mm/list_lru.c b/mm/list_lru.c
>> >index 7d0523e44010..fdb3fe2ea64f 100644
>> >--- a/mm/list_lru.c
>> >+++ b/mm/list_lru.c
>> >@@ -15,6 +15,14 @@
>> > #include "slab.h"
>> > #include "internal.h"
>> 
>> Hi, Johannes
>> 
>> One very tiny nit below.
>> 
>> > 
>> >+static inline void lock_list_lru(struct list_lru_one *l, bool irq)
>> 
>> Here we use @irq.
>> 
>> >+{
>> >+	if (irq)
>> >+		spin_lock_irq(&l->lock);
>> >+	else
>> >+		spin_lock(&l->lock);
>> >+}
>> >+
>> > static inline void unlock_list_lru(struct list_lru_one *l, bool irq_off)
>> 
>> Here we use @irq_off.
>> 
>> Do you think it would be nicer to unify the parameter name?
>
>Yes, I think it would be nicer.
>
>Note that I inherited this - we had irq on the lock and irq_off on the
>unlock before already. I didn't want to mix even more yak shaving prep
>patches into this series.

Reasonable.

>
>Mind sending a follow-up patch on top of mm-unstable?

Thanks, I am glad to.

Since this is trivial, I would wait until everything is settle down.

Looks good to you?

-- 
Wei Yang

2026-05-30.patch_summary: This patch deduplicates the lock_list_lru() function by removing redundant code and unifying parameter names to improve readability.
